$comment: Default hooks shipped by the flow plugin. NOT auto-applied — consumers opt-in by merging the desired hooks into their project's .claude/settings.json (PR 3 will ship a template/settings.json.example that does this by default). Two hooks ship: (1) sensitive-file write blocking, (2) path-validation for /flow:* tool invocations that take filesystem-path arguments (complements the in-script cwd constraint in extract_session.py).

PreToolUse.$comment: Hook 1: refuse to write to sensitive files. Matches Edit and Write tool calls; blocks (exit 2) if the file_path looks like a secret container. The plugin's own .claude/settings.json (project-dev side) uses this pattern; recommending it to consumers via this default hooks file.

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); FILE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [[ "$FILE" == *.env* ]] || [[ "$FILE" == *credentials* ]] || [[ "$FILE" == *secret* ]] || [[ "$FILE" == *.pem ]] || [[ "$FILE" == *_rsa ]]; then echo "Blocked by flow:default-hooks: refusing to write to sensitive file $FILE" >&2; exit 2; fi; exit 0

PreToolUse.$comment: Hook 2: warn when Bash invokes a /flow:* plugin tool with a --reference-paths-style argument that resolves outside cwd. Defense-in-depth against the PR-1 FOLLOW-UP #2 surface (extract_session.py --reference-paths reading arbitrary host paths). The in-script cwd constraint in plugins/flow/scripts/extract_session.py is the primary defense; this hook is the belt to its braces. Exits 0 (warning only, not blocking) to avoid breaking legitimate uses of the --allow-external-paths escape hatch; consumers who want hard enforcement can change exit 0 → exit 2.

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'plugins/flow/scripts/.*\.py.*--reference-paths\s+/' && ! echo "$CMD" | grep -qE -- '--allow-external-paths'; then echo "flow:default-hooks warning: plugin script invoked with absolute --reference-paths but no --allow-external-paths flag. Verify the paths are intentional." >&2; fi; exit 0
